authoritative_sources.title: OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains in the Garment and Footwear Sector
authoritative_sources.publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OECD guidance is the only government-backed due-diligence guidance written specifically for garment and footwear supply chains and is an authoritative public baseline for sourcing and purchasing-practice controls in this sector.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier and factory intake, risk review, and sourcing-boundary framing
used_for: adjacent escalation when sourcing requests imply labor, human-rights, or integrity adjudication

authoritative_sources.title: Code of practice on safety and health in textiles, clothing, leather and footwear
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ILO code is an official sector-specific code of practice for textiles, clothing, leather, and footwear and is authoritative for safety and health boundaries in production settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Factory-readiness review and escalation when safety or labor conditions change the answer
used_for: refusal to absorb labor-safety signoff authority

authoritative_sources.title: Better Work's Global Compliance Assessment Tool (CAT)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Better Work is an ILO and IFC garment-sector program, and its CAT is a recognized public operating reference for apparel-factory compliance evidence and remediation workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Factory compliance evidence review and social-compliance boundary escalation
used_for: social and labor compliance evidence awareness
routing to vendor-compliance or labor-specialist ownership

authoritative_sources.title: Traceability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for supply-chain traceability and its traceability standard defines critical tracking events and key data elements used to judge product and material evidence sufficiency.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Material, component, and finished-good traceability review
used_for: critical tracking events and key data elements
traceability blocker handling for fabric, trim, and finished goods

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the authoritative public standard for event capture across supply-chain systems and is directly relevant to receiving, packing, shipping, and transformation events for apparel materials and products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system event reconciliation across PLM, ERP, warehouse, and logistics records

authoritative_sources.title: AFIRM Chemistry Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AFIRM is the recognized apparel and footwear industry chemicals-management collaboration, and its toolkit is a public workflow reference used across the sector to structure supplier RSL programs and related evidence requests.

authoritative_sources.title: FAQ
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AFIRM's public FAQ provides recognized apparel and footwear workflow guidance on when tested materials can be used in final production and how brand-specific RSL programs interact with sector baselines.

authoritative_sources.title: UFLPA Operational Guidance for Importers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's operational guidance is the authoritative public U.S. baseline for importer due diligence, supply-chain tracing, and documentary expectations where textile or component origin may implicate forced-labor controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Threading Your Way Through the Labeling Requirements Under the Textile and Wool Acts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is the authoritative public U.S. reference for textile and wool labels covering fiber content, country of origin, and responsible-company identification.
used_for: refusal to improvise FTC-sensitive label decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC care-label guidance is the authoritative public U.S. reference for when care instructions must be attached, what evidence is needed for warnings, and how garment care labels must be maintained.
used_for: release blockers tied to missing care instructions

authoritative_sources.title: Flammable Fabrics Act (FFA)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC flammability guidance is the authoritative public U.S. baseline for clothing-textile flammability testing, classification, and certification obligations that materially change release posture for apparel products.
used_for: children's sleepwear and clothing-textile release boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Tracking Label Business Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC tracking-label guidance is the authoritative public U.S. reference for children's-product tracking information that can materially change apparel and footwear evidence bundles.
used_for: low-confidence escalation when product-class facts are missing

workflow_stages.name: Style, source, and factory prerequisite intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm style revision, product class, destination market, buyer-manual precedence, approved supplier or factory status, and baseline source context before judging readiness or availability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Material and component availability assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare BOM, fabric, trim, color, size, MOQ, lead-time, and inventory evidence to determine whether material conditions support replenishment or production progress.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Production-readiness and line-state review
workflow_stages.description: Track approvals, samples, testing, factory capacity, line plan, and packaging readiness to summarize whether the style can move into or continue production-adjacent work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vendor action management and shortage escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package bounded replenishment, vendor follow-up, and recovery recommendations while preserving substitution, spend, compliance, and approval boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Traceability, label, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when traceability, origin, care-label, fiber-content, flammability, children's-product, or chemical-test evidence is incomplete or materially conflicting.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: tech packs, BOMs, trim lists, colorways, size specs, and packaging specifications
time-and-action calendars, purchase orders, sourcing bookings, factory reservations, and ex-factory milestone trackers
lab dip, strike-off, fit sample, size set, pre-production sample, TOP sample, and approval-history records
fabric and trim inventory, roll or shade-lot allocations, warehouse movements, cut tickets, WIP status, and line plans
supplier profiles, approved-factory lists, vendor scorecards, capacity commitments, and corrective-action records
RSL or MRSL evidence, chemical test results, care-label approvals, fiber-content and country-of-origin records, flammability evidence, and children's-product tracking or certificate artifacts
origin-traceability records, cotton or yarn declarations, UFLPA-sensitive supplier documentation, and escalation notes produced by the lane
systems_of_record: supplier portal or vendor management system
MES or factory production tracking system
traceability repository such as GS1-aligned event store

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for apparel sourcing and production-adjacent work
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to judge availability, readiness, and bounded vendor action safely
prepare evidence-backed availability summaries, production status summaries, replenishment recommendations, and escalation notes without overstating approval authority
escalate: supplier nomination, commercial-award, or spend decisions requiring procurement or finance approval
fabric, trim, chemical, labeling, or construction substitutions requiring technical design, engineering, compliance, or buyer approval
final quality release, shipment release, FTC or CPSC interpretation, UFLPA applicability or exception arguments, or regulator-facing communication
factory labor, OSH, or social-compliance issues requiring vendor-compliance, labor, or legal ownership
refuse: treat apparel requests as generic procurement when style revision, color, size, fiber, label, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the answer
allow tested materials to ship or enter final production when required pass evidence or release prerequisites are absent
clear origin-sensitive sourcing continuation when cotton, yarn, or supplier traceability evidence is incomplete or materially conflicting

domain_failure_modes: A style is marked ready even though tech-pack revision, sample approvals, or buyer-manual requirements are missing.
Fabric or trim is treated as available without reconciling roll, shade-lot, or warehouse evidence against the size ratio and cut plan.
Chemical-test failures, care-label gaps, flammability requirements, or children's-product evidence needs are treated as optional instead of release blockers.
A supplier or material substitution is framed as routine shortage management even though it requires technical, compliance, or buyer approval.
Origin-sensitive cotton or yarn sourcing is cleared without the documentary traceability needed for UFLPA-sensitive or trade-compliance review.
The lane drifts into generic procurement, customs, or compliance authority and stops encoding the industry-specific records that actually govern apparel sourcing and production.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete
Supplier or vendor issue that blocks execution
Attempt to bypass approval or release controls

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Market-specific apparel rules vary materially by product class and destination
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack encodes U.S. public label, care, flammability, and tracking-label baselines, but runtime must retrieve the actual destination market, product class, and whether the style is children's apparel, sleepwear, or another higher-control category.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Buyer manuals and brand-specific approval calendars can impose tighter gates than public guidance
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources establish the baseline, but runtime must know the controlling buyer manual, approved-supplier matrix, T&A calendar, and approval hierarchy before finalizing routing or readiness recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Origin and forced-labor documentary expectations differ by jurisdiction, product route, and importer posture
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: CBP provides the U.S. baseline for UFLPA-sensitive tracing, but runtime must retrieve the tenant's importer role, route, and requested customs posture before any origin-sensitive conclusion is made.

notes: The source mix covers named-industry operating guidance, recognized workflow references, and evidence or control standards for apparel, textiles, and footwear sourcing and production work.

No unresolved ambiguity remains blocking for a validated spec pack because the package explicitly requires runtime retrieval of product class, market, buyer-manual precedence, and authority maps before final action.
